_README_NEW_CARGOS: How to add a custom cargo:
1. Add an object to the new_cargos list below.
2. copy_from = the vanilla cargo whose row gets cloned (template).
3. new_id    = the new row's name. Recipes reference this string.
4. display_source = OPTIONAL. Existing cargo whose StringTable label
your new cargo borrows in the mission UI. Defaults
to copy_from. Custom display labels need a separate
StringTable asset that's not yet wired up.
5. safety_dps = REQUIRED list of vanilla DP class names whose inputs
will accept this cargo. MT crashes on world load if a
cargo has zero vanilla consumers. Pick destinations
whose payout leakage is acceptable.
6. Every other key on the entry is a UE cargo-row field name. Field
names are case-sensitive and must match the vanilla
cargo struct exactly. Run `python import_cargo_data.py`
to extract a vanilla cargo dump under
CargoImport/cargos/catalog.json — copy values 1:1.
7. Recipes (in DP entries below) reference cargos by name. Vanilla and
new_id values are both valid; unknown names get logged
and dropped at registry-load time.

_NEW_CARGO_FIELDS._: Reference of every cargo-row field that the framework can set verbatim on a new_cargos entry. Type column matches the underlying UE property class — use the matching JSON literal type when you set a value. Vanilla examples are from the Fuel row.

_payment.PaymentPer1Km: Float — per-km payout. Only fires when MT's road-distance calc finds a marked road; offroad runs ignore this. Vanilla Fuel: 300.
_payment.PaymentPer1KmMultiplierByMaxWeight: Float — per-km value scales with the cargo's max weight by this factor. Vanilla Fuel: 2.
_payment.PaymentSqrtRatio: Float — sqrt-curve coefficient applied to the payout. 1.0 default; <1 flattens long-route payouts, >1 amplifies. Vanilla Fuel: 1.
_payment.PaymentSqrtRatioMinCapcity: Int — minimum capacity before the sqrt curve kicks in. Vanilla Fuel: 0. (Fractional JSON values get truncated; setter warns when that happens.)
_payment.BasePayment: Int64 — flat base payout independent of distance. THE offroad lever — bump this to make missions worth running on dirt roads. Vanilla Fuel: 100.
_payment.ExportPrice: Int — price when delivered to a Container_Export-style DP. Vanilla Fuel: 10.
_payment.ImportPrice: Int — price MT charges when this cargo is imported into a region. Vanilla Fuel: 10.
_payment.MaxDamagePaymentMultiplier: Float — payout cap for fully-damaged cargo (0..1). Vanilla Fuel: 0.1.
_payment.DamageBonusMultiplier: Float — bonus multiplier for delivering with no damage. Vanilla Fuel: 0.
_payment.ManualLoadingPayment: Int64 — extra pay when the player manually loads the cargo vs using a forklift. Vanilla Fuel: 0.

_mission_gen.SpawnProbability: Int — random/ambient mission generator weight. Higher = more frequent. Vanilla Fuel: 10.
_mission_gen.NumCargoMin: Int — minimum units per generated mission. Vanilla Fuel: 5.
_mission_gen.NumCargoMax: Int — maximum units per generated mission. Vanilla Fuel: 20.
_mission_gen.MinDeliveryDistance: Float — mission gen filter, minimum km between source and destination. Vanilla Fuel: 1.
_mission_gen.MaxDeliveryDistance: Float — mission gen filter, maximum km. 0 = unlimited. Vanilla Fuel: 0.

_physical.VolumeSize: Int — capacity-units the cargo occupies on a trailer. Vanilla Fuel: 1.
_physical.bAllowStacking: Bool — multiple units share one trailer slot. Vanilla Fuel: false.
_physical.bUseDamage: Bool — cargo accumulates damage from rough driving / collisions. Vanilla Fuel: false.
_physical.Fragile: Int — damage sensitivity (higher = breaks easier). Vanilla Fuel: 0.
_physical.bHoldingOffsetUsingItemBounds: Bool — render attachment offset uses the item's bounding box instead of a fixed pivot. Vanilla Fuel: false.

_timer.bTimer: Bool — whether the cargo has a delivery time limit / bonus. Vanilla Fuel: false.
_timer.BaseTimeSeconds: Float — baseline seconds for the timer. Vanilla Fuel: 0.
_timer.TimerBySpeedKPH: Float — timer scales with the player's average speed in km/h. Vanilla Fuel: 0.
_timer.TimerByRoadSpeedLimitRatio: Float — timer scales with the road's speed limit (1.0 = match the limit). Vanilla Fuel: 0.

_meta.bDepcreated: Bool — marks the cargo as legacy. May hide it from random mission gen depending on MT version. Vanilla Fuel: false.

_NOT_supported_yet._: These exist on every cargo row but require array/struct/enum/object handling that the generic setter doesn't do. Touching them needs a one-off code path in MTBPInjector.MutateCargos.
_NOT_supported_yet.CargoType: EnumProperty — single value (Box, Pallet, LiquidContainer, ...). Useful but not wired yet.
_NOT_supported_yet.CargoSpaceTypes: Array<EnumProperty> — list of trailer-bay categories the cargo fits (Tanker, Container, Flatbed, ...).
_NOT_supported_yet.InTimeTipRange: Vector2D struct — UI tooltip min/max for the timer feature.
_NOT_supported_yet.ActorClass: ObjectProperty — the BP that spawns when the cargo physicalizes. Inherited from copy_from; changing requires new-import resolution.
_NOT_supported_yet.DumpCargoSurfaceMesh / DumpCargoSurfaceM: ObjectProperty — visuals for dump-truck pile rendering.
_NOT_supported_yet.Colors: Array<StructProperty> — per-cargo color palette for paint variants.
_NOT_supported_yet.CargoFlags: Int bitmask — opaque, intentionally not exposed (no documented bit meanings).

new_cargos._: Fuelx2 round-trip test — every supported field set to a value DIFFERENT from vanilla Fuel so a downstream dump-cargo-row can confirm each one was applied. bDepcreated intentionally omitted (would hide the cargo from mission gen during the test).

_doc.purpose: Per-name delivery-point configuration. Scene placeholders named DeliveryPoint_<KEY> resolve to the entry under <KEY> here. Missing entries are logged and ignored.
_doc.naming: Scene placeholder asset_key 'DeliveryPoint_My_Cool_Spot' looks up KEY 'My_Cool_Spot'. The in-game label defaults to KEY with underscores replaced by spaces ('My Cool Spot'), capped at 14 chars (source label byte budget).
_doc.auto_derived: target_class + target_path are always auto-generated. source_class + source_actor default from the chosen `template` but can be overridden per entry for experimental clones.
_doc.validation: Cargo names + cargo types are cross-checked against CargoImport/cargos/. Invalid names produce a warning and are dropped from that recipe (don't crash the build). new_id values from the new_cargos list count as valid.

fields.label: Optional in-game name. Default: KEY with '_' -> ' '. Max 14 chars (source label byte budget — varies by template).
fields.template: Optional. Selects the vanilla DP class to clone. Default: 'farm' (Farm_Corn_C/CornFarm_2 — verified end-to-end). Other classes (Container_ExportImport_C, ComonDrop_C, Factory_*) have shown crashes / persistence issues; add new templates to bp_registry._TEMPLATES once validated.
fields.source_class: Optional override. Bypasses `template` to clone a specific BP class. Experimental — may not work depending on the class's transitive dependencies.
fields.source_actor: Optional override. Pairs with source_class. Must name a vanilla instance of source_class that exists in Jeju_World.umap.
fields.recipes: List of production configs. See _example below for every supported field.
fields.marker_color: RESERVED. Hex color for the world-map marker. Pipeline propagates the value but MTBPInjector mutation is not yet wired (see AGENTS.md 'Marker / Icon Mutation (Pending)').
fields.icon: RESERVED. /Game/... path for the marker icon. Same plumbing status as marker_color.
fields.output_storage_cap: RESERVED. Per-cargo max storage on this delivery point (vanilla default ~100). Lives on a separate MTDeliveryPointInventoryRatio actor, not on the BP CDO — needs targeted mutation. Pipeline propagates the value but MTBPInjector mutation pending.

recipe_fields.inputs: {Cargo: Count} — specific cargo names. Vanilla names from cargos/cargo_names.txt OR new_id values from the new_cargos list above.
recipe_fields.input_types: [Type] OR {Type: Count} — EDeliveryCargoType filter from cargos/types.txt. Accepts ANY cargo of that type.
recipe_fields.output_types: [Type] OR {Type: Count} — same shape as input_types.
recipe_fields.time_seconds: Float — ProductionTimeSeconds for one production cycle.

recipes._: TF2 is the consumer side of the boost test. Accepts both vanilla Fuel and the boosted Fuelx2 — both produce CornBox. Players can deliver either cargo here and compare the payouts.
TF1 spawns Fuelx2 out of thin air on a timer (no inputs needed). Pairs with TF2 as the explicit producer-consumer chain for the boosted variant.
Multi-cargo input + multi-cargo output, custom speed + time.
Multi-cargo input + multi-cargo output, custom speed + time.
Timer-only background production — no inputs, just outputs over time.
Cargo-TYPE filter (any LargePackage accepted as input). List form.
Cargo-type filter as map with counts.
Type-to-type recipe — accept any Sand-type cargo, produce any Concrete-type.
